RegistryItem = {\n    displayText() {\n      return Msg['DUPLICATE_BLOCK'];\n    },\n    preconditionFn(scope: Scope) {\n      const block = scope.block;\n      if (!block!.isInFlyout && block!.isDeletable() && block!.isMovable()) {\n        if (block!.isDuplicatable()) {\n          return 'enabled';\n        }\n        return 'disabled';\n      }\n      return 'hidden';\n    },\n    callback(scope: Scope) {\n      if (!scope.block) return;\n      const data = scope.block.toCopyData();\n      if (!data) return;\n      clipboard.paste(data, scope.block.workspace);\n    },\n    scopeType: ContextMenuRegistry.ScopeType.BLOCK,\n    id: 'blockDuplicate',\n    weight: 1,\n    associatedKeyboardShortcut: 'duplicate',\n  };\n  ContextMenuRegistry.registry.register(duplicateOption);\n}\n\n/**\n * Option to add or remove block-level comment.\n */\nexport function registerComment() {\n  const commentOption: RegistryItem = {\n    displayText(scope: Scope) {\n      if (scope.block!.hasIcon(CommentIcon.TYPE)) {\n        // If there's already a comment,  option is to remove.\n        return Msg['REMOVE_COMMENT'];\n      }\n      // If there's no comment yet, option is to add.\n      return Msg['ADD_COMMENT'];\n    },\n    preconditionFn(scope: Scope) {\n      const block = scope.block;\n      if (\n        block &&\n        !block.isInFlyout &&\n        block.workspace.options.comments &&\n        !block.isCollapsed() &&\n        block.isEditable() &&\n        // Either block already has a comment so let us remove it,\n        // or the block isn't just one full-block field block, which\n        // shouldn't be allowed to have comments as there's no way to read them.\n        (block.hasIcon(CommentIcon.TYPE) || !isFullBlockField(block))\n      ) {\n        return 'enabled';\n      }\n      return 'hidden';\n    },\n    callback(scope: Scope) {\n      const block = scope.block;\n      if (block && block.hasIcon(CommentIcon.TYPE)) {\n        block.setCommentText(null);\n      } else {\n        block!.setCommentText('');\n      }\n    },\n    scopeType: ContextMenuRegistry.ScopeType.BLOCK,\n    id: 'blockComment',\n    weight: 2,\n  };\n  ContextMenuRegistry.registry.register(commentOption);\n}\n\n/**\n * Option to inline variables.\n */\nexport function registerInline() {\n  const inlineOption: RegistryItem = {\n    displayText(scope: Scope) {\n      return scope.block!.getInputsInline()\n        ? Msg['EXTERNAL_INPUTS']\n        : Msg['INLINE_INPUTS'];\n    },\n    preconditionFn(scope: Scope) {\n      const block = scope.block;\n      if (!block!.isInFlyout && block!.isMovable() && !block!.isCollapsed()) {\n        for (let i = 1; i < block!.inputList.length; i++) {\n          // Only display this option if there are two value or dummy inputs\n          // next to each other.\n          if (\n            !(block!.inputList[i - 1] instanceof StatementInput) &&\n            !(block!.inputList[i] instanceof StatementInput)\n          ) {\n            return 'enabled';\n          }\n        }\n      }\n      return 'hidden';\n    },\n    callback(scope: Scope) {\n      scope.block!.setInputsInline(!scope.block!.getInputsInline());\n    },\n    scopeType: ContextMenuRegistry.ScopeType.BLOCK,\n    id: 'blockInline',\n    weight: 3,\n  };\n  ContextMenuRegistry.registry.register(inlineOption);\n}\n\n/**\n * Option to collapse or expand a block.\n */\nexport function registerCollapseExpandBlock() {\n  const collapseExpandOption: RegistryItem = {\n    displayText(scope: Scope) {\n      return scope.block!.isCollapsed()\n        ? Msg['EXPAND_BLOCK']\n        : Msg['COLLAPSE_BLOCK'];\n    },\n    preconditionFn(scope: Scope) {\n      const block = scope.block;\n      if (\n        !block!.isInFlyout &&\n        block!.isMovable() &&\n        block!.workspace.options.collapse\n      ) {\n        return 'enabled';\n      }\n      return 'hidden';\n    },\n    callback(scope: Scope) {\n      scope.block!.setCollapsed(!scope.block!.isCollapsed());\n    },\n    scopeType: ContextMenuRegistry.ScopeType.BLOCK,\n    id: 'blockCollapseExpand',\n    weight: 4,\n  };\n  ContextMenuRegistry.registry.register(collapseExpandOption);\n}\n\n/**\n * Option to disable or enable a block.\n */\nexport function registerDisable() {\n  const disableOption: RegistryItem = {\n    displayText(scope: Scope) {\n      return scope.block!.hasDisabledReason(MANUALLY_DISABLED)\n        ? Msg['ENABLE_BLOCK']\n        : Msg['DISABLE_BLOCK'];\n    },\n    preconditionFn(scope: Scope) {\n      const block = scope.block;\n      if (\n        !block!.isInFlyout &&\n        block!.workspace.options.disable &&\n        block!.isEditable()\n      ) {\n        // Determine whether this block is currently disabled for any reason\n        // other than the manual reason that this context menu item controls.\n        const disabledReasons = block!.getDisabledReasons();\n        const isDisabledForOtherReason =\n          disabledReasons.size >\n          (disabledReasons.has(MANUALLY_DISABLED) ? 1 : 0);\n\n        if (block!.getInheritedDisabled() || isDisabledForOtherReason) {\n          return 'disabled';\n        }\n        return 'enabled';\n      }\n      return 'hidden';\n    },\n    callback(scope: Scope) {\n      const block = scope.block;\n      const existingGroup = eventUtils.getGroup();\n      if (!existingGroup) {\n        eventUtils.setGroup(true);\n      }\n      block!.setDisabledReason(\n        !block!.hasDisabledReason(MANUALLY_DISABLED),\n        MANUALLY_DISABLED,\n      );\n      eventUtils.setGroup(existingGroup);\n    },\n    scopeType: ContextMenuRegistry.ScopeType.BLOCK,\n    id: 'blockDisable',\n    weight: 5,\n  };\n  ContextMenuRegistry.registry.register(disableOption);\n}\n\n/**\n * Option to delete a block.\n */\nexport function registerDelete() {\n  const deleteOption: RegistryItem = {\n    displayText(scope: Scope) {\n      const block = scope.block;\n      // Count the number of blocks that are nested in this block.\n      let descendantCount = block!.getDescendants(false).length;\n      const nextBlock = block!.getNextBlock();\n      if (nextBlock) {\n        // Blocks in the current stack would survive this block's deletion.\n        descendantCount -= nextBlock.getDescendants(false).length;\n      }\n      return descendantCount === 1\n        ? Msg['DELETE_BLOCK']\n        : Msg['DELETE_X_BLOCKS'].replace('%1', `${descendantCount}`);\n    },\n    preconditionFn(scope: Scope) {\n      if (!scope.block!.isInFlyout && scope.block!.isDeletable()) {\n        return 'enabled';\n      }\n      return 'hidden';\n    },\n    callback(scope: Scope) {\n      if (scope.block) {\n        getFocusManager().focusNode(scope.block);\n        scope.block.checkAndDelete();\n      }\n    },\n    scopeType: ContextMenuRegistry.ScopeType.BLOCK,\n    id: 'blockDelete',\n    weight: 6,\n    associatedKeyboardShortcut: 'delete',\n  };\n  ContextMenuRegistry.registry.register(deleteOption);\n}\n\n/**\n * Option to open help for a block.\n */\nexport function registerHelp() {\n  const helpOption: RegistryItem = {\n    displayText() {\n      return Msg['HELP'];\n    },\n    preconditionFn(scope: Scope) {\n      const block = scope.block;\n      const url =\n        typeof block!.helpUrl === 'function'\n          ? block!.helpUrl()\n          : block!.helpUrl;\n      if (url) {\n        return 'enabled';\n      }\n      return 'hidden';\n    },\n    callback(scope: Scope) {\n      scope.block!.showHelp();\n    },\n    scopeType: ContextMenuRegistry.ScopeType.BLOCK,\n    id: 'blockHelp',\n    weight: 7,\n  };\n  ContextMenuRegistry.registry.register(helpOption);\n}\n\n/** Registers an option for deleting a workspace comment. */\nexport function registerCommentDelete() {\n  const deleteOption: RegistryItem = {\n    displayText: () => Msg['REMOVE_COMMENT'],\n    preconditionFn(scope: Scope) {\n      return scope.comment?.isDeletable() ? 'enabled' : 'hidden';\n    },\n    callback(scope: Scope) {\n      eventUtils.setGroup(true);\n      scope.comment?.dispose();\n      eventUtils.setGroup(false);\n      scope.comment?.workspace.getAudioManager().play('delete');\n    },\n    scopeType: ContextMenuRegistry.ScopeType.COMMENT,\n    id: 'commentDelete',\n    weight: 6,\n    associatedKeyboardShortcut: 'delete',\n  };\n  ContextMenuRegistry.registry.register(deleteOption);\n}\n\n/** Registers an option for duplicating a workspace comment. */\nexport function registerCommentDuplicate() {\n  const duplicateOption: RegistryItem = {\n    displayText: () => Msg['DUPLICATE_COMMENT'],\n    preconditionFn(scope: Scope) {\n      return scope.comment?.isMovable() ? 'enabled' : 'hidden';\n    },\n    callback(scope: Scope) {\n      if (!scope.comment) return;\n      const data = scope.comment.toCopyData();\n      if (!data) return;\n      clipboard.paste(data, scope.comment.workspace);\n    },\n    scopeType: ContextMenuRegistry.ScopeType.COMMENT,\n    id: 'commentDuplicate',\n    weight: 1,\n    associatedKeyboardShortcut: 'duplicate',\n  };\n  ContextMenuRegistry.registry.register(duplicateOption);\n}\n\n/** Registers an option for adding a workspace comment to the workspace. */\nexport function registerCommentCreate() {\n  const createOption: RegistryItem = {\n    displayText: () => Msg['ADD_COMMENT'],\n    preconditionFn: (scope: Scope) => {\n      return scope.workspace?.isMutator ? 'hidden' : 'enabled';\n    },\n    callback: (\n      scope: Scope,\n      menuOpenEvent: Event,\n      menuSelectEvent: Event,\n      location: Coordinate,\n    ) => {\n      const workspace = scope.workspace;\n      if (!workspace) return;\n      eventUtils.setGroup(true);\n      const comment = new RenderedWorkspaceComment(workspace);\n      comment.moveTo(\n        svgMath.screenToWsCoordinates(\n          workspace,\n          new Coordinate(location.x, location.y),\n        ),\n      );\n      getFocusManager().focusNode(comment);\n      eventUtils.setGroup(false);\n    },\n    scopeType: ContextMenuRegistry.ScopeType.WORKSPACE,\n    id: 'commentCreate',\n    weight: 8,\n  };\n  ContextMenuRegistry.registry.register(createOption);\n}\n\n/** Registers all block-scoped context menu items. */\nfunction registerBlockOptions_() {\n  registerDuplicate();\n  registerComment();\n  registerInline();\n  registerCollapseExpandBlock();\n  registerDisable();\n  registerDelete();\n  registerHelp();\n}\n\n/** Registers all workspace comment related menu items. */\nexport function registerCommentOptions() {\n  registerCommentDuplicate();\n  registerCommentDelete();\n  registerCommentCreate();\n}\n\n/**\n * Registers all default context menu items. This should be called once per\n * instance of ContextMenuRegistry.\n *\n * @internal\n */\nexport function registerDefaultOptions() {\n  registerWorkspaceOptions_();\n  registerBlockOptions_();\n}\n\nregisterDefaultOptions();\n",null,"/**\n * @license\n * Copyright 2024 Google LLC\n * SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n */\n\n/**\n * @file Predicates for testing Abstract event subclasses based on\n * their .type properties.  These are useful because there are places\n * where it is not possible to use instanceof <ClassConstructor> tests\n * for type narrowing due to load ordering issues that would be caused\n * by the need to import (rather than just import type) the class\n * constructors in question.\n */\n\nimport type {Abstract} from './events_abstract.js';\nimport type {BlockChange} from './events_block_change.js';\nimport type {BlockCreate} from './events_block_create.js';\nimport type {BlockDelete} from './events_block_delete.js';\nimport type {BlockDrag} from './events_block_drag.js';\nimport type {BlockFieldIntermediateChange} from './events_block_field_intermediate_change.js';\nimport type {BlockMove} from './events_block_move.js';\nimport type {BubbleOpen} from './events_bubble_open.js';\nimport type {Click} from './events_click.js';\nimport type {CommentChange} from './events_comment_change.js';\nimport type {CommentCollapse} from './events_comment_collapse.js';\nimport type {CommentCreate} from './events_comment_create.js';\nimport type {CommentDelete} from './events_comment_delete.js';\nimport type {CommentDrag} from './events_comment_drag.js';\nimport type {CommentMove} from './events_comment_move.js';\nimport type {CommentResize} from './events_comment_resize.js';\nimport type {Selected} from === EventType.FINISHED_LOADING;\n}\n","/**\n * @license\n * Copyright 2025 Google LLC\n * SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n */\n\nimport type {IFocusableNode} from './interfaces/i_focusable_node.js';\nimport type {IFocusableTree} from './interfaces/i_focusable_tree.js';\nimport * as dom from './utils/dom.js';\nimport {FocusableTreeTraverser} from './utils/focusable_tree_traverser.js';\n\n/**\n * Type declaration for returning focus to FocusManager upon completing an\n * ephemeral UI flow (such as a dialog). Normally, the FocusManager will refocus\n * the previously-focused element. If callers do not wish for the FocusManager\n * to do so, they may call this method with `restoreFocus` set to false to\n * prevent automatic refocusing and leave focus where it is.\n *\n *\n * See FocusManager.takeEphemeralFocus for more details.\n */\nexport type ReturnEphemeralFocus = (restoreFocus?: boolean) => void;\n\n/**\n * Represents an IFocusableTree that has been registered for focus management in\n * FocusManager.\n */\nclass TreeRegistration {\n  /**\n   * Constructs a new TreeRegistration.\n   *\n   * @param tree The tree being registered.\n   * @param rootShouldBeAutoTabbable Whether the tree should have automatic\n   *     top-level tab management.\n   */\n  constructor(\n    readonly tree: IFocusableTree,\n    readonly rootShouldBeAutoTabbable: boolean,\n  ) {}\n}\n\n/**\n * A per-page singleton that manages Blockly focus across one or more\n * IFocusableTrees, and bidirectionally synchronizes this focus with the DOM.\n *\n * Callers that wish to explicitly change input focus for select Blockly\n * components on the page should use the focus functions in this manager.\n *\n * The manager is responsible for handling focus events from the DOM (which may\n * may arise from users clicking on page elements) and ensuring that\n * corresponding IFocusableNodes are clearly marked as actively/passively\n * highlighted in the same way that this would be represented with calls to\n * focusNode().\n */\nexport class FocusManager {\n  /**\n   * The CSS class assigned to IFocusableNode elements that presently have\n   * active DOM and Blockly focus.\n   *\n   * This should never be used directly. Instead, rely on FocusManager to ensure\n   * nodes have active focus (either automatically through DOM focus or manually\n   * through the various focus* methods provided by this class).\n   *\n   * It's recommended to not query using this class name, either. Instead, use\n   * FocusableTreeTraverser or IFocusableTree's methods to find a specific node.\n   */\n  static readonly ACTIVE_FOCUS_NODE_CSS_CLASS_NAME = 'blocklyActiveFocus';\n\n  /**\n   * The CSS class assigned to IFocusableNode elements that presently have\n   * passive focus (that is, they were the most recent node in their relative\n   * tree to have active focus--see ACTIVE_FOCUS_NODE_CSS_CLASS_NAME--and will\n   * receive active focus again if their surrounding tree is requested to become\n   * focused, i.e. using focusTree below).\n   *\n   * See ACTIVE_FOCUS_NODE_CSS_CLASS_NAME for caveats and limitations around\n   * using this constant directly (generally it never should need to be used).\n   */\n  static readonly PASSIVE_FOCUS_NODE_CSS_CLASS_NAME = 'blocklyPassiveFocus';\n\n  private focusedNode: IFocusableNode | null = null;\n  private previouslyFocusedNode: IFocusableNode | null = null;\n  private registeredTrees: Array<TreeRegistration> = [];\n\n  private currentlyHoldsEphemeralFocus: boolean = false;\n  private lockFocusStateChanges: boolean = false;\n  private recentlyLostAllFocus: boolean = false;\n  private isUpdatingFocusedNode: boolean = false;\n\n  /**\n   * Root element in which popovers (WidgetDiv, DropDownDiv) currently live.\n   */\n  private popoverFocusRoot?: HTMLElement;\n\n  /**\n   * Set of callbacks to invoke if the popover focus root loses focus.\n   */\n  private popoverFocusLossHandlers: Set<() => void> = new Set();\n\n  /**\n   * Handler for focusout in the popover focus root that selectively\n   * invokes the popover focus loss handlers if focus has truly transitioned\n   * outside of the focus root, and not e.g. to a different popover.\n   */\n  private popoverFocusOutHandler = (e: FocusEvent) => {\n    const target = e.relatedTarget;\n    if (\n      target === null ||\n      (target instanceof Node && !this.popoverFocusRoot?.contains(target))\n    ) {\n      for (const handler of this.popoverFocusLossHandlers) {\n        handler();\n      }\n    }\n  };\n\n  constructor(\n    addGlobalEventListener: (type: string, listener: EventListener) => void,\n  ) {\n    // Note that 'element' here is the element *gaining* focus.\n    const maybeFocus = (element: Element | EventTarget | null) => {\n      // Skip processing the event if the focused node is currently updating.\n      if (this.isUpdatingFocusedNode) return;\n\n      this.recentlyLostAllFocus = !element;\n      let newNode: IFocusableNode | null | undefined = null;\n      if (element instanceof HTMLElement || element instanceof SVGElement) {\n        // If the target losing or gaining focus maps to any tree, then it\n        // should be updated. Per the contract of findFocusableNodeFor only one\n        // tree should claim the element, so the search can be exited early.\n        for (const reg of this.registeredTrees) {\n          const tree = reg.tree;\n          newNode = FocusableTreeTraverser.findFocusableNodeFor(element, tree);\n          if (newNode) break;\n        }\n      }\n\n      if (newNode && newNode.canBeFocused()) {\n        const newTree = newNode.getFocusableTree();\n        const oldTree = this.focusedNode?.getFocusableTree();\n        if (newNode === newTree.getRootFocusableNode() && newTree !== oldTree) {\n          // If the root of the tree is the one taking focus (such as due to\n          // being tabbed), try to focus the whole tree explicitly to ensure the\n          // correct node re-receives focus.\n          this.focusTree(newTree);\n        } else {\n          this.focusNode(newNode);\n        }\n      } else {\n        this.defocusCurrentFocusedNode();\n      }\n    };\n\n    // Register root document focus listeners for tracking when focus leaves all\n    // tracked focusable trees. Note that focusin and focusout can be somewhat\n    // overlapping in the information that they provide. This is fine because\n    // they both aim to check for focus changes on the element gaining or having\n    // received focus, and maybeFocus should behave relatively deterministic.\n    addGlobalEventListener('focusin', (event) => {\n      if (!(event instanceof FocusEvent)) return;\n\n      // When something receives focus, always use the current active element as\n      // the source of truth.\n      maybeFocus(document.activeElement);\n    });\n    addGlobalEventListener('focusout', (event) => {\n      if (!(event instanceof FocusEvent)) return;\n\n      // When something loses focus, it seems that document.activeElement may\n      // not necessarily be correct. Instead, use relatedTarget.\n      maybeFocus(event.relatedTarget);\n    });\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Registers a new IFocusableTree for automatic focus management.\n   *\n   * If the tree currently has an element with DOM focus, it will not affect the\n   * internal state in this manager until the focus changes to a new,\n   * now-monitored element/node.\n   *\n   * This function throws if the provided tree is already currently registered\n   * in this manager. Use isRegistered to check in cases when it can't be\n   * certain whether the tree has been registered.\n   *\n   * The tree's registration can be customized to configure automatic tab stops.\n   * This specifically provides capability for the user to be able to tab\n   * navigate to the root of the tree but only when the tree doesn't hold active\n   * focus. If this functionality is disabled then the tree's root will\n   * automatically be made focusable (but not tabbable) when it is first focused\n   * in the same way as any other focusable node.\n   *\n   * @param tree The IFocusableTree to register.\n   * @param rootShouldBeAutoTabbable Whether the root of this tree should be\n   *     added as a top-level page tab stop when it doesn't hold active focus.\n   */\n  registerTree(\n    tree: IFocusableTree,\n    rootShouldBeAutoTabbable: boolean = false,\n  ): void {\n    this.ensureManagerIsUnlocked();\n    if (this.isRegistered(tree)) {\n      throw Error(`Attempted to re-register already registered tree: ${tree}.`);\n    }\n    this.registeredTrees.push(\n      new TreeRegistration(tree, rootShouldBeAutoTabbable),\n    );\n    const rootElement = tree.getRootFocusableNode().getFocusableElement();\n    if (!rootElement.id || rootElement.id === 'null') {\n      throw Error(\n        `Attempting to register a tree with a root element that has an ` +\n          `invalid ID: ${tree}.`,\n      );\n    }\n    if (rootShouldBeAutoTabbable) {\n      rootElement.tabIndex = 0;\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Returns whether the specified tree has already been registered in this\n   * manager using registerTree and hasn't yet been unregistered using\n   * unregisterTree.\n   */\n  isRegistered(tree: IFocusableTree): boolean {\n    return !!this.lookUpRegistration(tree);\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Returns the TreeRegistration for the specified tree, or null if the tree is\n   * not currently registered.\n   */\n  private lookUpRegistration(tree: IFocusableTree): TreeRegistration | null {\n    return this.registeredTrees.find((reg) => reg.tree === tree) ?? null;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Unregisters a IFocusableTree from automatic focus management.\n   *\n   * If the tree had a previous focused node, it will have its highlight\n   * removed. This function does NOT change DOM focus.\n   *\n   * This function throws if the provided tree is not currently registered in\n   * this manager.\n   *\n   * This function will reset the tree's root element tabindex if the tree was\n   * registered with automatic tab management.\n   */\n  unregisterTree(tree: IFocusableTree): void {\n    this.ensureManagerIsUnlocked();\n    if (!this.isRegistered(tree)) {\n      throw Error(`Attempted to unregister not registered tree: ${tree}.`);\n    }\n    const treeIndex = this.registeredTrees.findIndex(\n      (reg) => reg.tree === tree,\n    );\n    const registration = this.registeredTrees[treeIndex];\n    this.registeredTrees.splice(treeIndex, 1);\n\n    const focusedNode = FocusableTreeTraverser.findFocusedNode(tree);\n    const root = tree.getRootFocusableNode();\n    if (focusedNode) this.removeHighlight(focusedNode);\n    if (this.focusedNode === focusedNode || this.focusedNode === root) {\n      this.updateFocusedNode(null);\n    }\n    this.removeHighlight(root);\n\n    if (registration.rootShouldBeAutoTabbable) {\n      tree\n        .getRootFocusableNode()\n        .getFocusableElement()\n        .removeAttribute('tabindex');\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Returns the current IFocusableTree that has focus, or null if none\n   * currently do.\n   *\n   * Note also that if ephemeral focus is currently captured (e.g. using\n   * takeEphemeralFocus) then the returned tree here may not currently have DOM\n   * focus.\n   */\n  getFocusedTree(): IFocusableTree | null {\n    return this.focusedNode?.getFocusableTree() ?? null;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Returns the current IFocusableNode with focus (which is always tied to a\n   * focused IFocusableTree), or null if there isn't one.\n   *\n   * Note that this function will maintain parity with\n   * IFocusableTree.getFocusedNode(). That is, if a tree itself has focus but\n   * none of its non-root children do, this will return null but\n   * getFocusedTree() will not.\n   *\n   * Note also that if ephemeral focus is currently captured (e.g. using\n   * takeEphemeralFocus) then the returned node here may not currently have DOM\n   * focus.\n   */\n  getFocusedNode(): IFocusableNode | null {\n    return this.focusedNode;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Focuses the specific IFocusableTree. This either means restoring active\n   * focus to the tree's passively focused node, or focusing the tree's root\n   * node.\n   *\n   * Note that if the specified tree already has a focused node then this will\n   * not change any existing focus (unless that node has passive focus, then it\n   * will be restored to active focus).\n   *\n   * See getFocusedNode for details on how other nodes are affected.\n   *\n   * @param focusableTree The tree that should receive active\n   *     focus.\n   */\n  focusTree(focusableTree: IFocusableTree): void {\n    this.ensureManagerIsUnlocked();\n    if (!this.isRegistered(focusableTree)) {\n      throw Error(`Attempted to focus unregistered tree: ${focusableTree}.`);\n    }\n    const currNode = FocusableTreeTraverser.findFocusedNode(focusableTree);\n    const nodeToRestore = focusableTree.getRestoredFocusableNode(currNode);\n    const rootFallback = focusableTree.getRootFocusableNode();\n    this.focusNode(nodeToRestore ?? currNode ?? rootFallback);\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Focuses DOM input on the specified node, and marks it as actively focused.\n   *\n   * Any previously focused node will be updated to be passively highlighted (if\n   * it's in a different focusable tree) or blurred (if it's in the same one).\n   *\n   * **Important**: If the provided node is not able to be focused (e.g. its\n   * canBeFocused() method returns false), it will be ignored and any existing\n   * focus state will remain unchanged.\n   *\n   * Note that this may update the specified node's element's tabindex to ensure\n   * that it can be properly read out by screenreaders while focused.\n   *\n   * The focused node will not be automatically scrolled into view.\n   *\n   * @param focusableNode The node that should receive active focus.\n   */\n  focusNode(focusableNode: IFocusableNode): void {\n    this.ensureManagerIsUnlocked();\n    const mustRestoreUpdatingNode = !this.currentlyHoldsEphemeralFocus;\n    if (mustRestoreUpdatingNode) {\n      // Disable state syncing from DOM events since possible calls to focus()\n      // below will loop a call back to focusNode().\n      this.isUpdatingFocusedNode = true;\n    }\n\n    // Double check that state wasn't desynchronized in the background. See:\n    // https://github.com/google/blockly-keyboard-experimentation/issues/87.\n    // This is only done for the case where the same node is being focused twice\n    // since other cases should automatically correct (due to the rest of the\n    // routine running as normal).\n    const prevFocusedElement = this.focusedNode?.getFocusableElement();\n    const hasDesyncedState = prevFocusedElement !== document.activeElement;\n    if (this.focusedNode === focusableNode && !hasDesyncedState) {\n      if (mustRestoreUpdatingNode) {\n        // Reenable state syncing from DOM events.\n        this.isUpdatingFocusedNode = false;\n      }\n      return; // State is unchanged.\n    }\n\n    if (!focusableNode.canBeFocused()) {\n      // This node can't be focused.\n      console.warn(\"Trying to focus a node that can't be focused.\");\n\n      if (mustRestoreUpdatingNode) {\n        // Reenable state syncing from DOM events.\n        this.isUpdatingFocusedNode = false;\n      }\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const nextTree = focusableNode.getFocusableTree();\n    if (!this.isRegistered(nextTree)) {\n      throw Error(`Attempted to focus unregistered node: ${focusableNode}.`);\n    }\n\n    const focusableNodeElement = focusableNode.getFocusableElement();\n    if (!focusableNodeElement.id || focusableNodeElement.id === 'null') {\n      // Warn that the ID is invalid, but continue execution since an invalid ID\n      // will result in an unmatched (null) node. Since a request to focus\n      // something was initiated, the code below will attempt to find the next\n      // best thing to focus, instead.\n      console.warn('Trying to focus a node that has an invalid ID.');\n    }\n\n    // Safety check for ensuring focusNode() doesn't get called for a node that\n    // isn't actually hooked up to its parent tree correctly. This usually\n    // happens when calls to focusNode() interleave with asynchronous clean-up\n    // operations (which can happen due to ephemeral focus and in other cases).\n    // Fall back to a reasonable default since there's no valid node to focus.\n    const matchedNode = FocusableTreeTraverser.findFocusableNodeFor(\n      focusableNodeElement,\n      nextTree,\n    );\n    const prevNodeNextTree = FocusableTreeTraverser.findFocusedNode(nextTree);\n    let nodeToFocus = focusableNode;\n    if (matchedNode !== focusableNode) {\n      const nodeToRestore = nextTree.getRestoredFocusableNode(prevNodeNextTree);\n      const rootFallback = nextTree.getRootFocusableNode();\n      nodeToFocus = nodeToRestore ?? prevNodeNextTree ?? rootFallback;\n    }\n\n    const prevNode = this.focusedNode;\n    const prevTree = prevNode?.getFocusableTree();\n    if (prevNode) {\n      this.passivelyFocusNode(prevNode, nextTree);\n    }\n\n    // If there's a focused node in the new node's tree, ensure it's reset.\n    const nextTreeRoot = nextTree.getRootFocusableNode();\n    if (prevNodeNextTree) {\n      this.removeHighlight(prevNodeNextTree);\n    }\n    // For caution, ensure that the root is always reset since getFocusedNode()\n    // is expected to return null if the root was highlighted, if the root is\n    // not the node now being set to active.\n    if (nextTreeRoot !== nodeToFocus) {\n      this.removeHighlight(nextTreeRoot);\n    }\n\n    if (!this.currentlyHoldsEphemeralFocus) {\n      // Only change the actively focused node if ephemeral state isn't held.\n      this.activelyFocusNode(nodeToFocus, prevTree ?? null);\n    }\n    this.updateFocusedNode(nodeToFocus);\n    if (mustRestoreUpdatingNode) {\n      // Reenable state syncing from DOM events.\n      this.isUpdatingFocusedNode = false;\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Ephemerally captures focus for a specific element until the returned lambda\n   * is called. This is expected to be especially useful for ephemeral UI flows\n   * like dialogs.\n   *\n   * IMPORTANT: the returned lambda *must* be called, otherwise automatic focus\n   * will no longer work anywhere on the page. It is highly recommended to tie\n   * the lambda call to the closure of the corresponding UI so that if input is\n   * manually changed to an element outside of the ephemeral UI, the UI should\n   * close and automatic input restored. Note that this lambda must be called\n   * exactly once and that subsequent calls will throw an error.\n   *\n   * Note that the manager will continue to track DOM input signals even when\n   * ephemeral focus is active, but it won't actually change node state until\n   * the returned lambda is called. Additionally, only 1 ephemeral focus context\n   * can be active at any given time (attempting to activate more than one\n   * simultaneously will result in an error being thrown).\n   *\n   * This method does not scroll the ephemerally focused element into view.\n   */\n  takeEphemeralFocus(\n    focusableElement: HTMLElement | SVGElement,\n  ): ReturnEphemeralFocus {\n    this.ensureManagerIsUnlocked();\n    if (this.currentlyHoldsEphemeralFocus) {\n      throw Error(\n        `Attempted to take ephemeral focus when it's already held, ` +\n          `with new element: ${focusableElement}.`,\n      );\n    }\n    this.currentlyHoldsEphemeralFocus = true;\n\n    if (this.focusedNode) {\n      this.passivelyFocusNode(this.focusedNode, null);\n    }\n    focusableElement.focus({preventScroll: true});\n\n    let hasFinishedEphemeralFocus = false;\n    return (restoreFocus = true) => {\n      if (hasFinishedEphemeralFocus) {\n        throw Error(\n          `Attempted to finish ephemeral focus twice for element: ` +\n            `${focusableElement}.`,\n        );\n      }\n      hasFinishedEphemeralFocus = true;\n      this.currentlyHoldsEphemeralFocus = false;\n      if (this.focusedNode && restoreFocus) {\n        this.activelyFocusNode(this.focusedNode, null);\n\n        // Even though focus was restored, check if it's lost again. It's\n        // possible for the browser to force focus away from all elements once\n        // the ephemeral element disappears. This ensures focus is restored.\n        const capturedNode = this.focusedNode;\n        setTimeout(() => {\n          // These checks are set up to minimize the risk that a legitimate\n          // focus change occurred within the delay that this would override.\n          if (\n            !this.focusedNode &&\n            this.previouslyFocusedNode === capturedNode &&\n            this.recentlyLostAllFocus\n          ) {\n            this.focusNode(capturedNode);\n          }\n        }, 0);\n      }\n    };\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * @returns whether something is currently holding ephemeral focus\n   */\n  ephemeralFocusTaken(): boolean {\n    return this.currentlyHoldsEphemeralFocus;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Ensures that the manager is currently allowing operations that change its\n   * internal focus state (such as via focusNode()).\n   *\n   * If the manager is currently not allowing state changes, an exception is\n   * thrown.\n   */\n  private ensureManagerIsUnlocked(): void {\n    if (this.lockFocusStateChanges) {\n      throw Error(\n        'FocusManager state changes cannot happen in a tree/node focus/blur ' +\n          'callback.',\n      );\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Updates the internally tracked focused node to the specified node, or null\n   * if focus is being lost. This also updates previous focus tracking.\n   *\n   * @param newFocusedNode The new node to set as focused.\n   */\n  private updateFocusedNode(newFocusedNode: IFocusableNode | null) {\n    this.previouslyFocusedNode = this.focusedNode;\n    this.focusedNode = newFocusedNode;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Defocuses the current actively focused node tracked by the manager, iff\n   * there's a node being tracked and the manager doesn't have ephemeral focus.\n   */\n  private defocusCurrentFocusedNode(): void {\n    // The current node will likely be defocused while ephemeral focus is held,\n    // but internal manager state shouldn't change since the node should be\n    // restored upon exiting ephemeral focus mode.\n    if (this.focusedNode && !this.currentlyHoldsEphemeralFocus) {\n      this.passivelyFocusNode(this.focusedNode, null);\n      this.updateFocusedNode(null);\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Marks the specified node as actively focused, also calling related\n   * lifecycle callback methods for both the node and its parent tree. This\n   * ensures that the node is properly styled to indicate its active focus.\n   *\n   * This does not change the manager's currently tracked node, nor does it\n   * change any other nodes.\n   *\n   * @param node The node to be actively focused.\n   * @param prevTree The tree of the previously actively focused node, or null\n   *     if there wasn't a previously actively focused node.\n   */\n  private activelyFocusNode(\n    node: IFocusableNode,\n    prevTree: IFocusableTree | null,\n  ): void {\n    // Note that order matters here. Focus callbacks are allowed to change\n    // element visibility which can influence focusability, including for a\n    // node's focusable element (which *is* allowed to be invisible until the\n    // node needs to be focused).\n    this.lockFocusStateChanges = true;\n    const tree = node.getFocusableTree();\n    const elem = node.getFocusableElement();\n    const nextTreeReg = this.lookUpRegistration(tree);\n    const treeIsTabManaged = nextTreeReg?.rootShouldBeAutoTabbable;\n    if (tree !== prevTree) {\n      tree.onTreeFocus(node, prevTree);\n\n      if (treeIsTabManaged) {\n        // If this node's tree has its tab auto-managed, ensure that it's no\n        // longer tabbable now that it holds active focus.\n        tree.getRootFocusableNode().getFocusableElement().tabIndex = -1;\n      }\n    }\n    node.onNodeFocus();\n    this.lockFocusStateChanges = false;\n\n    // The tab index should be set in all cases where:\n    // - It doesn't overwrite an pre-set tab index for the node.\n    // - The node is part of a tree whose tab index is unmanaged.\n    // OR\n    // - The node is part of a managed tree but this isn't the root. Managed\n    //   roots are ignored since they are always overwritten to have a tab index\n    //   of -1 with active focus so that they cannot be tab navigated.\n    //\n    // Setting the tab index ensures that the node's focusable element can\n    // actually receive DOM focus.\n    if (!treeIsTabManaged || node !== tree.getRootFocusableNode()) {\n      if (!elem.hasAttribute('tabindex')) elem.tabIndex = -1;\n    }\n\n    this.setNodeToVisualActiveFocus(node);\n    elem.focus({preventScroll: true});\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Marks the specified node as passively focused, also calling related\n   * lifecycle callback methods for both the node and its parent tree. This\n   * ensures that the node is properly styled to indicate its passive focus.\n   *\n   * This does not change the manager's currently tracked node, nor does it\n   * change any other nodes.\n   *\n   * @param node The node to be passively focused.\n   * @param nextTree The tree of the node receiving active focus, or null if no\n   *     node will be actively focused.\n   */\n  private passivelyFocusNode(\n    node: IFocusableNode,\n    nextTree: IFocusableTree | null,\n  ): void {\n    this.lockFocusStateChanges = true;\n    const tree = node.getFocusableTree();\n    if (tree !== nextTree) {\n      tree.onTreeBlur(nextTree);\n\n      const reg = this.lookUpRegistration(tree);\n      if (reg?.rootShouldBeAutoTabbable) {\n        // If this node's tree has its tab auto-managed, ensure that it's now\n        // tabbable since it no longer holds active focus.\n        tree.getRootFocusableNode().getFocusableElement().tabIndex = 0;\n      }\n    }\n    node.onNodeBlur();\n    this.lockFocusStateChanges = false;\n\n    if (tree !== nextTree) {\n      this.setNodeToVisualPassiveFocus(node);\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Updates the node's styling to indicate that it should have an active focus\n   * indicator.\n   *\n   * @param node The node to be styled for active focus.\n   */\n  private setNodeToVisualActiveFocus(node: IFocusableNode): void {\n    const element = node.getFocusableElement();\n    dom.addClass(element, FocusManager.ACTIVE_FOCUS_NODE_CSS_CLASS_NAME);\n    dom.removeClass(element, FocusManager.PASSIVE_FOCUS_NODE_CSS_CLASS_NAME);\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Updates the node's styling to indicate that it should have a passive focus\n   * indicator.\n   *\n   * @param node The node to be styled for passive focus.\n   */\n  private setNodeToVisualPassiveFocus(node: IFocusableNode): void {\n    const element = node.getFocusableElement();\n    dom.removeClass(element, FocusManager.ACTIVE_FOCUS_NODE_CSS_CLASS_NAME);\n    dom.addClass(element, FocusManager.PASSIVE_FOCUS_NODE_CSS_CLASS_NAME);\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Removes any active/passive indicators for the specified node.\n   *\n   * @param node The node which should have neither passive nor active focus\n   *     indication.\n   */\n  private removeHighlight(node: IFocusableNode): void {\n    const element = node.getFocusableElement();\n    dom.removeClass(element, FocusManager.ACTIVE_FOCUS_NODE_CSS_CLASS_NAME);\n    dom.removeClass(element, FocusManager.PASSIVE_FOCUS_NODE_CSS_CLASS_NAME);\n  }\n\n  private static focusManager: FocusManager | null = null;\n\n  /**\n   * Returns the page-global FocusManager.\n   *\n   * The returned instance is guaranteed to not change across function calls,\n   * but may change across page loads.\n   */\n  static getFocusManager(): FocusManager {\n    if (!FocusManager.focusManager) {\n      FocusManager.focusManager = new FocusManager(document.addEventListener);\n    }\n    return FocusManager.focusManager;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Sets the current popover focus root. Generally this is active\n   * workspace's injection div or the explicitly specified parent container for\n   * the WidgetDiv, DropDownDiv, etc.\n   *\n   * @internal\n   * @param newRoot The new element that contains all popovers.\n   */\n  setPopoverFocusRoot(newRoot: HTMLElement) {\n    this.popoverFocusRoot?.removeEventListener(\n      'focusout',\n      this.popoverFocusOutHandler,\n    );\n    this.popoverFocusRoot = newRoot;\n    this.popoverFocusRoot.addEventListener(\n      'focusout',\n      this.popoverFocusOutHandler,\n    );\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Registers a callback to be invoked if the popover focus root loses\n   * focus. This should only be called by popovers that need to react to\n   * focus changes by e.g. hiding themselves and resigning ephemeral focus.\n   *\n   * @internal\n   * @param handler A callback function.\n   */\n  registerPopoverFocusLossHandler(handler: () => void) {\n    this.popoverFocusLossHandlers.add(handler);\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Unregisters a previously-registered popover focus loss handler. This\n   * should only be invoked by popovers when they no longer need to be\n   * notified of focus loss, typically when they are hidden.\n   *\n   * @internal\n   * @param handler A previously-registered callback function.\n   */\n  unregisterPopoverFocusLossHandler(handler: () => void) {\n    this.popoverFocusLossHandlers.delete(handler);\n  }\n}\n\n/** Convenience function for FocusManager.getFocusManager. */\nexport function getFocusManager(): FocusManager {\n  return FocusManager.getFocusManager();\n}\n","/**\n * @license\n * Copyright 2016 Google LLC\n * SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n */\n\n// Former goog.module ID: Blockly.constants\n\n/**\n * The language-neutral ID given to the collapsed input.\n */\nexport const COLLAPSED_INPUT_NAME = '_TEMP_COLLAPSED_INPUT';\n\n/**\n * The language-neutral ID given to the collapsed field.\n */\nexport const COLLAPSED_FIELD_NAME = '_TEMP_COLLAPSED_FIELD';\n\n/**\n * The language-neutral ID for when the reason why a block is disabled is\n * because the user manually disabled it, such as via the context menu.\n */\nexport const MANUALLY_DISABLED = 'MANUALLY_DISABLED';\n","/**\n * @license\n * Copyright 2020 Google LLC\n * SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n */\n\n// Former goog.module ID: Blockly.IContextMenu\n\nexport interface IContextMenu {\n  /**\n   * Show the context menu for this object.\n   *\n   * @param e Mouse event.\n   */\n  showContextMenu(e: Event): void;\n}\n\n/** @returns true if the given object implements IContextMenu. */\nexport function hasContextMenu(obj: any): obj is IContextMenu {\n  return obj && typeof obj.showContextMenu === 'function';\n}\n",null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,"/**\n * @license\n * Copyright 2013 Google LLC\n * SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n */\n\n// Former goog.module ID: Blockly.blocks\n\n/**\n * A block definition. Its constants enumerate\n * all SVG tag names used by Blockly.\n *\n * @class\n */\n// Former goog.module ID: Blockly.utils.Svg\n\n/**\n * A name with the type of the SVG element stored in the generic.\n */\nexport class Svg<_T> {\n  /** @internal */\n  static ANIMATE = new Svg<SVGAnimateElement>('animate');\n  /** @internal */\n  static CIRCLE = new Svg<SVGCircleElement>('circle');\n  /** @internal */\n  static CLIPPATH = new Svg<SVGClipPathElement>('clipPath');\n  /** @internal */\n  static DEFS = new Svg<SVGDefsElement>('defs');\n  /** @internal */\n  static FECOMPOSITE = new from '../interfaces/i_focusable_node.js';\nimport type {IFocusableTree} from '../interfaces/i_focusable_tree.js';\nimport * as dom from '../utils/dom.js';\n\n/**\n * A helper utility for IFocusableTree implementations to aid with common\n * tree traversals.\n */\nexport class FocusableTreeTraverser {\n  private static readonly ACTIVE_CLASS_NAME = 'blocklyActiveFocus';\n  private static readonly PASSIVE_CSS_CLASS_NAME = 'blocklyPassiveFocus';\n  private static readonly ACTIVE_FOCUS_NODE_CSS_SELECTOR = `.${FocusableTreeTraverser.ACTIVE_CLASS_NAME}`;\n  private static readonly PASSIVE_FOCUS_NODE_CSS_SELECTOR = `.${FocusableTreeTraverser.PASSIVE_CSS_CLASS_NAME}`;\n\n  /**\n   * Returns the current IFocusableNode that is styled (and thus represented) as\n   * having either passive or active focus, only considering HTML and SVG\n   * elements.\n   *\n   * This can match against the tree's root.\n   *\n   * Note that this will never return a node from a nested sub-tree as that tree\n   * should specifically be used to retrieve its focused node.\n   *\n   * @param tree The IFocusableTree in which to search for a focused node.\n   * @returns The IFocusableNode currently with focus, or null if none.\n   */\n  static findFocusedNode(tree: IFocusableTree): IFocusableNode | null {\n    const rootNode = tree.getRootFocusableNode();\n    if (!rootNode.canBeFocused()) return null;\n    const root = rootNode.getFocusableElement();\n    if (\n      dom.hasClass(root, FocusableTreeTraverser.ACTIVE_CLASS_NAME) ||\n      dom.hasClass(root, FocusableTreeTraverser.PASSIVE_CSS_CLASS_NAME)\n    ) {\n      // The root has focus.\n      return rootNode;\n    }\n\n    const activeEl = root.querySelector(this.ACTIVE_FOCUS_NODE_CSS_SELECTOR);\n    if (activeEl instanceof HTMLElement || activeEl instanceof SVGElement) {\n      const active = FocusableTreeTraverser.findFocusableNodeFor(\n        activeEl,\n        tree,\n      );\n      if (active) return active;\n    }\n\n    // At most there should be one passive indicator per tree (not considering\n    // subtrees).\n    const passiveEl = root.querySelector(this.PASSIVE_FOCUS_NODE_CSS_SELECTOR);\n    if (passiveEl instanceof HTMLElement || passiveEl instanceof SVGElement) {\n      const passive = FocusableTreeTraverser.findFocusableNodeFor(\n        passiveEl,\n        tree,\n      );\n      if (passive) return passive;\n    }\n\n    return null;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Returns the IFocusableNode corresponding to the specified HTML or SVG\n   * element iff it's the root element or a descendent of the root element of\n   * the specified IFocusableTree.\n   *\n   * If the element exists within the specified tree's DOM structure but does\n   * not directly correspond to a node, the nearest parent node (or the tree's\n   * root) will be returned to represent the provided element.\n   *\n   * If the tree contains another nested IFocusableTree, the nested tree may be\n   * traversed but its nodes will never be returned here per the contract of\n   * IFocusableTree.lookUpFocusableNode.\n   *\n   * The provided element must have a non-null, non-empty ID that conforms to\n   * the contract mentioned in IFocusableNode.\n   *\n   * @param element The HTML or SVG element being sought.\n   * @param tree The tree under which the provided element may be a descendant.\n   * @returns The matching IFocusableNode, or null if there is no match.\n   */\n  static findFocusableNodeFor(\n    element: HTMLElement | SVGElement,\n    tree: IFocusableTree,\n  ): IFocusableNode | null {\n    // Note that the null check is due to Element.setAttribute() converting null\n    // to a string.\n    if (!element.id || element.id === 'null') return null;\n\n    // First, match against subtrees.\n    const subTreeMatches = tree.getNestedTrees().map((tree) => {\n      return FocusableTreeTraverser.findFocusableNodeFor(element, tree);\n    });\n    if (subTreeMatches.findIndex((match) => !!match) !== -1) {\n      // At least one subtree has a match for the element so it cannot be part\n      // of the outer tree.\n      return null;\n    }\n\n    // Second, check against the tree's root.\n    const rootNode = tree.getRootFocusableNode();\n    if (rootNode.canBeFocused() && element === rootNode.getFocusableElement()) {\n      return rootNode;\n    }\n\n    // Third, check if the element has a node.\n    const matchedChildNode = tree.lookUpFocusableNode(element.id) ?? null;\n    if (matchedChildNode) return matchedChildNode;\n\n    // Fourth, recurse up to find the nearest tree/node if it's possible.\n    const elementParent = element.parentElement;\n    if (!matchedChildNode && elementParent) {\n      return FocusableTreeTraverser.findFocusableNodeFor(elementParent, tree);\n    }\n\n    // Otherwise, there's no matching node.\n    return null;\n  }\n}\n","/**\n * @license\n * Copyright 2020 Google LLC\n * SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n */\n\n/**\n * The namespace used to keep track of keyboard shortcuts and the\n * key codes used to execute those shortcuts.\n *\n * @class\n */\n// Former goog.module ID: Blockly.ShortcutRegistry\n\nimport {Scope} from './contextmenu_registry.js';\nimport {getFocusManager} from './focus_manager.js';\nimport {KeyCodes} from './utils/keycodes.js';\nimport * as object from './utils/object.js';\nimport {WorkspaceSvg} from './workspace_svg.js';\n\n/**\n * Class for the registry of keyboard shortcuts. This is intended to be a\n * singleton. You should not create a new instance, and only access this class\n * from ShortcutRegistry.registry.\n */\nexport class ShortcutRegistry {\n  static readonly registry = new ShortcutRegistry();\n\n  /** Registry of all keyboard shortcuts, keyed by name of shortcut. */\n  private shortcuts = new Map<string, KeyboardShortcut>();\n\n  /** Map of key codes to an array of shortcut names. */\n  private keyMap = new Map<string, string[]>();\n\n  /** Resets the existing ShortcutRegistry singleton. */\n  private constructor() {\n    this.reset();\n  }\n\n  /** Clear and recreate the registry and keyMap. */\n  reset() {\n    this.shortcuts.clear();\n    this.keyMap.clear();\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Registers a keyboard shortcut.\n   *\n   * @param shortcut The shortcut for this key code.\n   * @param allowOverrides True to prevent a warning when overriding an\n   *     already registered item.\n   * @throws {Error} if a shortcut with the same name already exists.\n   */\n  register(shortcut: KeyboardShortcut, allowOverrides?: boolean) {\n    const registeredShortcut = this.shortcuts.get(shortcut.name);\n    if (registeredShortcut && !allowOverrides) {\n      throw new Error(`Shortcut named \"${shortcut.name}\" already exists.`);\n    }\n    this.shortcuts.set(shortcut.name, shortcut);\n\n    const keyCodes = shortcut.keyCodes;\n    if (keyCodes?.length) {\n      for (const keyCode of keyCodes) {\n        this.addKeyMapping(keyCode, shortcut.name, !!shortcut.allowCollision);\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Unregisters a keyboard shortcut registered with the given name. This\n   * will also remove any key mappings that reference this shortcut.\n   *\n   * @param shortcutName The name of the shortcut to unregister.\n   * @returns True if an item was unregistered, false otherwise.\n   */\n  unregister(shortcutName: string): boolean {\n    const shortcut = this.shortcuts.get(shortcutName);\n\n    if (!shortcut) {\n      console.warn(`Keyboard shortcut named \"${shortcutName}\" not found.`);\n      return false;\n    }\n\n    this.removeAllKeyMappings(shortcutName);\n\n    this.shortcuts.delete(shortcutName);\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Adds a mapping between a keycode and a keyboard shortcut.\n   *\n   * Normally only one shortcut can be mapped to any given keycode,\n   * but setting allowCollisions to true allows a keyboard to be\n   * mapped to multiple shortcuts.  In that case, when onKeyDown is\n   * called with the given keystroke, it will process the mapped\n   * shortcuts in reverse order, from the most- to least-recently\n   * mapped).\n   *\n   * @param keyCode The key code for the keyboard shortcut. If registering a key\n   *     code with a modifier (ex: ctrl+c) use\n   *     ShortcutRegistry.registry.createSerializedKey;\n   * @param shortcutName The name of the shortcut to execute when the given\n   *     keycode is pressed.\n   * @param allowCollision True to prevent an error when adding a shortcut\n   *     to a key that is already mapped to a shortcut.\n   * @throws {Error} if the given key code is already mapped to a shortcut.\n   */\n  addKeyMapping(\n    keyCode: string | number | KeyCodes,\n    shortcutName: string,\n    allowCollision?: boolean,\n  ) {\n    keyCode = `${keyCode}`;\n    const shortcutNames = this.keyMap.get(keyCode);\n    if (shortcutNames && !allowCollision) {\n      throw new Error(\n        `Shortcut named \"${shortcutName}\" collides with shortcuts \"${shortcutNames}\"`,\n      );\n    } else if (shortcutNames && allowCollision) {\n      shortcutNames.unshift(shortcutName);\n    } else {\n      this.keyMap.set(keyCode, [shortcutName]);\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Removes a mapping between a keycode and a keyboard shortcut.\n   *\n   * @param keyCode The key code for the keyboard shortcut. If registering a key\n   *     code with a modifier (ex: ctrl+c) use\n   *     ShortcutRegistry.registry.createSerializedKey;\n   * @param shortcutName The name of the shortcut to execute when the given\n   *     keycode is pressed.\n   * @param quiet True to not console warn when there is no shortcut to\n   *     remove.\n   * @returns True if a key mapping was removed, false otherwise.\n   */\n  removeKeyMapping(\n    keyCode: string,\n    shortcutName: string,\n    quiet?: boolean,\n  ): boolean {\n    const shortcutNames = this.keyMap.get(keyCode);\n\n    if (!shortcutNames) {\n      if (!quiet) {\n        console.warn(\n          `No keyboard shortcut named \"${shortcutName}\" registered with key code \"${keyCode}\"`,\n        );\n      }\n      return false;\n    }\n\n    const shortcutIdx = shortcutNames.indexOf(shortcutName);\n    if (shortcutIdx > -1) {\n      shortcutNames.splice(shortcutIdx, 1);\n      if (shortcutNames.length === 0) {\n        this.keyMap.delete(keyCode);\n      }\n      return true;\n    }\n    if (!quiet) {\n      console.warn(\n        `No keyboard shortcut named \"${shortcutName}\" registered with key code \"${keyCode}\"`,\n      );\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Removes all the key mappings for a shortcut with the given name.\n   * Useful when changing the default key mappings and the key codes registered\n   * to the shortcut are unknown.\n   *\n   * @param shortcutName The name of the shortcut to remove from the key map.\n   */\n  removeAllKeyMappings(shortcutName: string) {\n    for (const keyCode of this.keyMap.keys()) {\n      this.removeKeyMapping(keyCode, shortcutName, /* quiet= */ true);\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Sets the key map. Setting the key map will override any default key\n   * mappings.\n   *\n   * @param newKeyMap The object with key code to shortcut names.\n   */\n  setKeyMap(newKeyMap: {[key: string]: string[]}) {\n    this.keyMap.clear();\n    for (const key in newKeyMap) {\n      this.keyMap.set(key, newKeyMap[key]);\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Gets the current key map.\n   *\n   * @returns The object holding key codes to ShortcutRegistry.KeyboardShortcut.\n   */\n  getKeyMap(): {[key: string]: string[]} {\n    const legacyKeyMap: {[key: string]: string[]} = Object.create(null);\n    for (const [key, value] of this.keyMap) {\n      legacyKeyMap[key] = value;\n    }\n    return legacyKeyMap;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Gets the registry of keyboard shortcuts.\n   *\n   * @returns The registry of keyboard shortcuts.\n   */\n  getRegistry(): {[key: string]: KeyboardShortcut} {\n    const legacyRegistry: {[key: string]: KeyboardShortcut} =\n      Object.create(null);\n    for (const [key, value] of this.shortcuts) {\n      legacyRegistry[key] = value;\n    }\n    return object.deepMerge(Object.create(null), legacyRegistry);\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Handles key down events.\n   *\n   * - Any `KeyboardShortcut`(s) mapped to the keycodes that cause\n   *   event `e` to be fired will be processed, in order from least-\n   *   to most-recently registered.\n   * - If the shortcut's `preconditionFn` exists it will be called.\n   *   If `preconditionFn` returns false the shortcut's `callback`\n   *   function will be skipped.  Processing will continue with the\n   *   next shortcut, if any.\n   * - The shortcut's `callback` function will then be called.  If it\n   *   returns true, processing will terminate and `onKeyDown` will\n   *   return true.  If it returns false, processing will continue\n   *   with with the next shortcut, if any.\n   * - If all registered shortcuts for the given keycode have been\n   *   processed without any having returned true, `onKeyDown` will\n   *   return false.\n   *\n   * @param workspace The main workspace where the event was captured.\n   * @param e The key down event.\n   * @returns True if the event was handled, false otherwise.\n   */\n  onKeyDown(workspace: WorkspaceSvg, e: KeyboardEvent): boolean {\n    const key = this.serializeKeyEvent(e);\n    const shortcutNames = this.getShortcutNamesByKeyCode(key);\n    if (!shortcutNames) return false;\n    for (const shortcutName of shortcutNames) {\n      const shortcut = this.shortcuts.get(shortcutName);\n      if (\n        !shortcut ||\n        (shortcut.preconditionFn &&\n          !shortcut.preconditionFn(workspace, {\n            focusedNode: getFocusManager().getFocusedNode() ?? undefined,\n          }))\n      ) {\n        continue;\n      }\n      // If the key has been handled, stop processing shortcuts.\n      if (\n        shortcut.callback?.(workspace, e, shortcut, {\n          focusedNode: getFocusManager().getFocusedNode() ?? undefined,\n        })\n      ) {\n        return true;\n      }\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Gets the shortcuts registered to the given key code.\n   *\n   * @param keyCode The serialized key code.\n   * @returns The list of shortcuts to call when the given keyCode is used.\n   *     Undefined if no shortcuts exist.\n   */\n  getShortcutNamesByKeyCode(keyCode: string): string[] | undefined {\n    // Copy the list of shortcuts in case one of them unregisters itself\n    // in its callback.\n    return this.keyMap.get(keyCode)?.slice() || [];\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Gets the serialized key codes that the shortcut with the given name is\n   * registered under.\n   *\n   * @param shortcutName The name of the shortcut.\n   * @returns An array with all the key codes the shortcut is registered under.\n   */\n  getKeyCodesByShortcutName(shortcutName: string): string[] {\n    const keys = [];\n    for (const [keyCode, shortcuts] of this.keyMap) {\n      const shortcutIdx = shortcuts.indexOf(shortcutName);\n      if (shortcutIdx > -1) {\n        keys.push(keyCode);\n      }\n    }\n    return keys;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Serializes a key event.\n   *\n   * @param e A key down event.\n   * @returns The serialized key code for the given event.\n   */\n  private serializeKeyEvent(e: KeyboardEvent): string {\n    let serializedKey = '';\n    for (const modifier in ShortcutRegistry.modifierKeys) {\n      if (e.getModifierState(modifier)) {\n        if (serializedKey !== '') {\n          serializedKey += '+';\n        }\n        serializedKey += modifier;\n      }\n    }\n    if (serializedKey !== '' && e.keyCode) {\n      serializedKey += '+' + e.keyCode;\n    } else if (e.keyCode) {\n      serializedKey = String(e.keyCode);\n    }\n    return serializedKey;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Checks whether any of the given modifiers are not valid.\n   *\n   * @param modifiers List of modifiers to be used with the key.\n   * @throws {Error} if the modifier is not in the valid modifiers list.\n   */\n  private checkModifiers(modifiers: KeyCodes[]) {\n    for (const modifier of modifiers) {\n      if (!(modifier in ShortcutRegistry.modifierKeys)) {\n        throw new Error(modifier + ' is not a valid modifier key.');\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Creates the serialized key code that will be used in the key map.\n   *\n   * @param keyCode Number code representing the key.\n   * @param modifiers List of modifier key codes to be used with the key. All\n   *     valid modifiers can be found in the `ShortcutRegistry.modifierKeys`.\n   * @returns The serialized key code for the given modifiers and key.\n   */\n  createSerializedKey(keyCode: number, modifiers: KeyCodes[] | null): string {\n    let serializedKey = '';\n\n    if (modifiers) {\n      this.checkModifiers(modifiers);\n      for (const modifier in ShortcutRegistry.modifierKeys) {\n        const modifierKeyCode = (\n          ShortcutRegistry.modifierKeys as AnyDuringMigration\n        )[modifier];\n        if (modifiers.includes(modifierKeyCode)) {\n          if (serializedKey !== '') {\n            serializedKey += '+';\n          }\n          serializedKey += modifier;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n\n    if (serializedKey !==
